Technical Deep Dive

From electrical signal to validated diagnostic

SAM4 turns raw current and voltage waveforms into confirmed fault diagnostics — continuously, for your entire fleet. This page walks through the full pipeline: how signals are captured, how AI models detect anomalies, how engineers validate every alert, and how results reach your team.

10,000+
Assets monitored today
1,874
Resolved incidents (Jan 2024 – Feb 2026)
0.32%
False communication rate
€80M+
Detection value identified
Request a Demo
01 Signal Acquisition
02 Signal Processing
03 AI Detection
04 Engineer Validation
05 Scheduled Work Order
STEP 01

Signal acquisition at the MCC

Every AC motor draws current and voltage in patterns that reflect the mechanical condition of the full drivetrain. SAM4 captures those patterns at the motor control cabinet — not on the asset.

Hardware

Split-core CTs and voltage taps clamp around motor supply cables inside the MCC. Brief de-energisation at MCC. Back online same shift. No mechanical modification. Under 60 minutes per asset.

Sampling

The SAM4 DAQ unit samples current and voltage at up to 96 kHz, capturing harmonic content needed to detect mechanical faults reflected in the electrical signature.

Edge processing

Raw waveforms are pre-processed on the edge device: windowing, quality checks, compression. Only diagnostic-relevant data sent to cloud.

Connectivity

Data transmits over 4G, Ethernet, or Wi-Fi. Edge device handles intermittent connectivity, buffering locally. No IT infrastructure changes required.

Why the MCC, not the asset?

Conventional vibration sensors need physical mounting — which means access, permits, wiring, and per-asset costs. For submerged pumps, sealed blowers, ATEX zones, the MCC is always accessible and serves every motor on the feeder.

STEP 02

Signal processing and feature extraction

Raw electrical waveforms contain thousands of data points per second. The signal processing layer transforms this into structured diagnostic features — the fingerprint of each asset's condition.

Spectral decomposition

Time-domain signals transformed to frequency domain using FFT. Reveals harmonic patterns: sideband frequencies around supply frequency that correspond to specific mechanical faults.

Feature extraction

Hundreds of diagnostic features extracted: spectral amplitudes at fault-specific frequencies, envelope statistics, current signature symmetry, power factor trends, load-normalised indicators.

Operating state classification

Motors operate across loads, speeds, duty cycles. SAM4 classifies each measurement's operating state before comparing to baseline. Critical for VFD-driven or intermittent assets.

Baseline learning

During initial monitoring, SAM4 builds a statistical baseline per asset across observed operating states. Every motor evaluated relative to its own normal behaviour.

STEP 03

AI-driven anomaly detection

The detection engine compares each asset's current condition against its learned baseline and a library of known fault patterns — trained on data from 10,000+ industrial assets across 80+ customers.

Anomaly scoring

Each feature vector scored against baseline using statistical distance metrics. System tracks trend direction and rate of change, distinguishing stable anomaly from accelerating failure.

Fault classification

Classification models map deviating features to known fault types: bearing, alignment, balance, electrical, cavitation. Trained on labelled fault data from confirmed failures — real-world ground truth, not lab data.

Severity estimation

Beyond detecting a fault, the engine estimates how far degradation has progressed. Combines spectral deviation magnitude, trend progression rate, and fault-specific degradation models.

Model improvement

Every validated alert feeds back into training. With 80+ customers generating continuous feedback, detection accuracy compounds over time. Scale creates an advantage.

STEP 04

Every alert reviewed by a reliability engineer

AI flags the anomaly. A human confirms it. Every alert that reaches your team has been reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer.

AI flags anomaly

Detection engine identifies statistically significant deviation. Preliminary classification with confidence score.

Engineer reviews

Reliability engineer examines spectral data, reviews operating context, confirms or refines fault classification. Assigns severity: routine, planned, or urgent. Writes specific maintenance recommendation with timeline.

Critical escalation

Rapidly progressing faults escalated immediately. Your team gets a phone call, not just a dashboard update.

Alert delivered

Validated diagnostics published to SAM4 dashboard with full evidence: spectral plots, trend history, fault classification, severity, recommended action.

Why human-in-the-loop?

Fully automated systems generate alert fatigue. Teams stop trusting them. SAM4's hybrid approach means every alert carries engineer judgement — which is why customers act on them.

STEP 05

Alert delivery and system integration

Validated diagnostics reach your team through the SAM4 dashboard and push directly into your existing maintenance systems.

Single-asset detail — health indicators, failure timeline, and recommended maintenance actions

SAM4 Dashboard

Fleet-level overview with drill-down. Each diagnostic includes fault classification, severity, trend history, spectral evidence, recommended action.

CMMS Integration

Pushes alerts into SAP PM, Maximo, Ultimo as work orders. No manual re-entry. Integrates directly into existing maintenance workflows without process change.

REST API

Pull asset health data, alert history, trend metrics into custom dashboards or data platforms.

Alert Taxonomy

Structured severity: Watch (monitor), Warning (plan intervention), Critical (act now). Defined SLA per level.

Reporting

Monthly fleet health reports. Quarterly business reviews mapping findings to outcomes: confirmed saves, avoided downtime, efficiency gains.

Detection accuracy across 10,000+ assets

These metrics are measured across confirmed failures in the installed base, not lab conditions.

90.8%
Detection rate (core assets). 1,874 resolved incidents (Jan 2024 – Feb 2026)
0.32%
False communication rate. 6 out of 1,874 resolved incidents, fewer than 1 in 300.
Weeks–months
Lead time before failure. Varies by fault type and asset.
24/7
Continuous monitoring. No scheduled visits or campaigns.

How we measure detection performance

Definitions matter. We publish ours.

True Positive (TP)
SAM4 finding confirmed by customer action, inspection, or observed failure.
False Positive (FP)
SAM4 finding not confirmed as a real fault.
False Negative (FN)
Asset failure without prior SAM4 alert.
Detection Rate
TP / (TP + FN) = 90.8% (n=1,874, Jan 2024 – Feb 2026)
False Positive Rate
FP / (TP + FP) = 0.32% (n=1,874)

Transparency

We publish this metric because we believe condition monitoring vendors should define their terms. If a vendor quotes an accuracy rate without stating the formula, sample, and time window — ask them to.

What these numbers don't tell you

  • Detection varies by fault type. Blockage and mechanical unbalance highest. Bearing detection lower (45.9% overall) due to signal attenuation between bearing and MCC.
  • Lead time isn't guaranteed. SAM4 is not trip protection.
  • Baseline calibration takes 2 days to 2 weeks. Novel asset types not yet in the model library may require longer.

Enterprise-grade data handling

Data ownership

Your data remains yours. Raw data and results available for export. Retention policies contractually defined.

Cloud infrastructure

Encryption at rest and in transit. Role-based access control. ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certified. NIS2 aligned.

Network requirements

Outbound-only connectivity. No inbound ports, no VPN, no firewall changes. Minimal bandwidth after edge compression.

Go deeper or get started

Whether you're evaluating the technology or ready to see it on your assets.
